examples: Hagfish have a mesonephric kidney and are often neotenic of their pronephric kidney.
They're kind of like eels or hagfish or lampreys, but different.
The hagfish, unfortunately, sometimes falls prey to its own defense mechanism, but normally it twists itself into knots to escape the gelatinous goop.
No-one has made a spool of hagfish thread yet, but scientists are working on it.
Instead, scientists hope to make proteins like the ones found in hagfish slime artificially in the lab.
